His Adapted mom does learn of a kryptonite like weakness the character has. But does she wait too long to attempt to stop the savage carnage being ravaged by her beloved adapter son?  See for yourselves



Well produced and intense film. Better than I expected.

text:
Oooh, the prequel to BvS has arrived!

In all seriousness though, it’s funny how this is being hailed as something new and creative (and I’m really excited to see it!) when in many ways it is one of the more logical ends for a kid with superpowers?

Imagine growing up, being treated like a messiah figure by your parents. You weren’t born on earth so you likely grow up exhibiting likely aspergers or autistic-like symptoms, and when other kids start to bully you for it, you realize you have superpowers. You’re strong, you can fly, you can shoot lasers from your eyes. No other kid can do that. Mom always said you were special.
You start dreaming of the place that you called home, memories creeping in. You were raised in a militaristic society. Blood and death defined your birth. Mom and dad lied to you. You’re not human, you never were.
They start to realize you’re not human. They start to get scared, wondering if you’re a monster. A demon instead of an angel. So you start to wonder the same.
You’re not human. You never were. So why do you have to follow their rules? Your people believed in war, believed in winning battles through bloodshed. Soon enough, you’re locking the doors to the school and blowing it up. You’re murdering the woman who called you a freak in the principal’s office. You’re chasing your father, and you’re chasing your mother. 
They all deserve it. Because they lied. They called you a hero, a saviour, but why would you save the world when its people have shown you again and again that they’re just not worth it?

text: This is basically how Smallville Season 8 depicted Davis Bloome as Doomsday to be.. He wasnt found by the Kents and raised with the same values as Clark aka Superman.. Instead he was found by Lionel Luther Lex's dad only to be thrown out in the streets and forced to grow on his own. This is simply showing how even if the Kents had found Davis and raised him he'd still turn out as Doomsday destined to kill Clark. A sort of twisted Mirror Box type movie and I LOVE IT! What I always wanted in a film. A superman horror story. Maybe it'll collide with DC and introduce a New universe where they can redeem themselves because JL, Aquaman, Shazam.. All not so great at all.

text:
The scariest thing is that THIS is how Superman would most likely turn out if he really did exist.

When you find out you're the most powerful being on the planet and no one to tell you what to do, you're more likely to turn into an amoral sociopath than the unrealistically idealistic boy scout that Superman is known as.

Anyone who's read Irredeemable or Alan Moore's run on Miracleman will know what I mean.

text:
An idea for an evil Batman: 
The son of a rich couple sees his parents been murdered by the Cosa Nostra (the villains in the Tim Burton movie). He later leaves to travel the world, looking for training. He eventually joining an order of Professional killers dedicated to deliver back alley justice. He desserts and uses his skills for vengeance rather than justice. The boy discovers a a taste for violence, and once his targets are dead, he decides to continue with all others. 

Some have deducted his identity, but his power and influence made him untouchable. There is still someone willing to investigate his crimes, a war veteran turned detective. 

The boy is now a man. He wares a dark costume and is usually silent.... except when he laughs like a demon clown. 


The main inspirations would be Nemesis by Mark Millar, Patrick Bateman from American Psycho and the Batman who laughs.

text:
I honestly feel that something is off about the trailer.
1. In Trailer, kid hasn't killed anyone (yet)
2. His Face hasn't been revealed yet
So, my theory is that there is an evil twin here that is doing this killings. Coz whenever he (the cape kid) supposedly killed someone in the trailer, we didn't get to see his face just a mask. And whenever the kid was shown in the trailer he never had blood on him at any part of his body. My guess is that he has a brother out there and somehow they are telepathically connected or the kid's suffering from split personality disorder. I know this is not much to support my theory but its all i got, and for those who are thinking "But the kid was sitting inside the crashed plane" I feel that was a misdirection. Please dont reply to this comment. If you like it then press like and if you dont give a dislike or just ignore after all its free

text:
This is obviously a twist on the Superman Story, what if he would turn to bad?  
The big difference here are  the adoptive and caring parents.  Martha and Jonathan Kent, in Superman's case,  they both instituted morals on his adoptive son.  There is an excellent scene in Man of Still when a young Clark Kent is overwhelmed by his out of control senses that he runs from his classroom and hides in a closet, he hears his classmates calling him a freak then his mother Martha came to help him get out of the closet. 
With great powers comes great responsibility, remember who told that to Spiderman?  His uncle Ben, who was a good and honest man and Peter changed when he failed to save him by letting a delincuent scape when he could have stopped him.  
In the TV series Smallville, a teen aged Clark Kent also spent a period of time abusing his power.  And here is the real problem:  Power corrupts, absolute power absolutely corrupts, so it would be easier to accept that a powerful human like alien would turn to bad rather than to good, because being good is hard and being bad is fun.  But this notion is getting out of hand in this depiction, bordeline in sadistic on this Brightburn movie.
You see a protective mother which is logical coming from a woman who could not bear children.  There is also a scene where she tells him that he is a blessing, so you wonder what made him change into evil, to abuse his power when his mother seems very good.  Also the scene with the lawn mower it just is simply ridiculous, no one on his right mind would try to stop the blade with his hand, if you want to know if you are strong or bulletproof you would start with basics, a needle, a knife, hitting a wall, but what if you were wrong and while trying to stop the blade your fingers get cut off?  Ooops!

A sec later, it says.. 
"Earth,Another Dimension."

In a rainy night, a boy is sketching something in his notebook.Right after he s done sketching,a Thunder strikes to the ground.He Shocked and looks outside the window, quietly.
Suddenly he heard a lady called him from downstairs. 
"Honey, dinner is ready!"
"Coming mom!"he answered and once again looks at the outside the window before walks away, leaving the notebook open. The camera slowly approaching the notebook and guess what...a familiar logo slowly shows up but just when the its almost show us what it is,the screen black.

and a manly voice said
"We will meet... soon.. "

Credits.

text:
Finally! A true "superhero" movie! THIS is exactly what a "superbeing" would turn up to be if they came to Earth. 
All that BS in Superman's story is...well...BS. No being with this huge amount of power would be "nice & good". There's simply no way.
It doesn't matter if it's human or alien. If "it" is raised "as a human being" it's going to have the same experiences and way of thinking as a real human. And there's no way someone wouldn't eventually abuse of so much power. Especially as a teenager.

So the hype is real! Can't wait for #Brightburn.  >:)

text: I'm definitely watching this movie! I love the premise. Although i can't get behind the idea of people calling him "evil". He's NOT evil. What would happen if you were bullied as a child by everyone at school, called a creep and even the teachers didn't try to help you? What if you suddenly discovered that you had super powers? In this scenario, can any of us honestly say they wouldn't get revenge? We, as normal humans, typically don't because we are weak. We can't fly, put our hand in a running lawnmower blade and not have it cut off, or effortlessly crush someones hand by taking it. Not to mention the laser eyes! I'm confident that regardless of what we say we would do in such a situation, EVERYONE would most likely use these powers for their own gain and soon wouldn't care about the greater good or humanities "justice". Besides, "good" and "evil' are points of view. People are extremely complex and attempting to define someone with a single word is just absurd. But i digress, this is going to be a great movie! So looking forward to it!!!

text:
If you don't have a relationship with God, it is essential that today you acknowledge that without Him you are lost and are spiritually dead.  Then you need to repent, which means to express genuine godly sorrow for your sins and turn from them, for they have deeply grieved and angered God.   
Turn to God, believe in and receive His Son Jesus Christ, Who shed His own blood and sacrificially died on the cross for your sins.  Believe that three days later Jesus Christ rose from the dead.  When you do this salvation is yours, otherwise when you die you will go to hell for eternity.  God and Jesus will send the Holy Spirit to live in you, to grow you as a Christian, which means "Christ follower".  He will lead you to live holy, to live righteously in fear of God with a heart and a spirit that is transformed to do good works which please God and Jesus.  He will also lead you to a Bible believing, preaching church.  
God loves you, He wants to forgive you, and He wants to give you everlasting life in Heaven, but, only you can make that choice to seek His mercy & change how you live by freely accepting His gracious offer.

text:
The potential behind this movie is so incredibly thick.

They could totally pull a Venom and make this a prequel to the Dark Superman arch type from the Injustice game that eventually rules the world. The current superman we know would have to face him and instead of typical super hero villain dynamic, we'll actually get to visit the psychological aspects of why the original superman doesn't do the same thing to fix the world. We could visit deep dogmas and zeitgeists throughout the film. It could be dark and gritty and beautiful and get us into the mind of a god. 

Fingers crossed.

author: @AdamF90

text:
Could be interesting if the writing's good, but, once again the stereotype of the bullied kid turning evil gets played out by Hollywood, so that leaves me with my doubts. Look at the majority of serial killers, dictators, genocidal rulers. Notice what they all have in common? The ability to mask themselves as competent, often popular members of society. American Psycho is much closer to the mark in this regard.

Not saying the idea here is totally bad, it just relies to heavily on a really terrible Hollywood trope which does more to reinforce bullying. It'd be far more interesting if the character seemed normal to everyone else and we get to see their other side and their horrible rationalisations (or maybe they don't even have any) for their actions. This one's a hard pass for me at the cinema.

text: I was never a fan of superhero films. As a kid, I felt they ignored logic too much, and basically insulted intelligence because it was a genre and concept that was somehow allowed to get away with so much. Spiderman, Hulk and Superman were my usual go-to when explaining my reasoning... and this is basically how I described it would really go down if an alien baby with extreme amounts of power was to be raised by humans in rural America. This is exactly what I spoke about when talking about Superman. I am very excited to see that someone else shares the more disturbingly likely scenario that I always imagined, but I was bullied for saying as I was either too nerdy, too depressing, or just felt that I was ruining things for the sake of it.

तो आपकी कम्पनी कहां से पैसा कमाएगी और आपको कहां से देगी तो सोचो समझो और फिर करो और लगातार करते रहो
तो अाप कहोगे कि निरंतरता ठीक होती है भले ही शुरु मे आाय कम हो नहीं तो अपना जीवन यू ही ख़राब करते रहेंगे।
*क्या आप बार बार असफल हो रहे तो रुके .....✋🏻🤔*
*कहीँ आप ये तो नहीँ सोच रहे की आप दुनिया के सबसे समझदार व्यक्ति है।*

text:
Imagine how many more horror superhero movies can be made...
A boy bitten by a radioactive spider as he slowly morphs into a monstrous human-spider hybrid who eats people...
A rich kid who's parents were killed in front of him, thus making him go crazy and dress up as he uses his wealth and intellect to cause pain and destruction...
A young amazon girl who discovers man's world as the violent conquering amazons use their superior strength and intellect to try and take over man's world...
A weapons manufacturer who was injured in a foreign country and used his technology to rebuild himself. But something went wrong in the programming as he becomes more machine then human...
A new group of humans pop up who are gifted with incredible abilities who are discriminated against. A new group of these "people" come together not to save people, but to make humanity pay for the pain and discrimination they went through...
Anyone else got ideas?

text:
CAPTAIN MARVEL ( FEMINIST) THE MOVIE:
In 1995, on the Kree Empire's capital planet of Hala, warrior Vers suffers from recurring nightmares involving a battle and an older woman while training to join Starforce. Her mentor, Yon-Rogg, warns her to control her ability to fire photon blasts from her hands. When she loses her temper, Vers meets with the Supreme Intelligence, an artificial intelligence and the Kree's ruler, who offers the same warning.

Yon-Rogg leads Vers and fellow Starforce members Att-Lass, Bron-Char, Korath and Minerva to a planet infiltrated by the Skrulls, a race of alien shapeshifters that can perfectly simulate anyone, and with whom the Kree are at war. When attempting to extricate a captured spy, Vers is taken prisoner and subjected to a memory probe by the Skrulls' General Talos. Escaping with Talos and others in pursuit, she reaches Earth — crashing through a Blockbuster Video store in Los Angeles. She rigs a communication device to contact Starforce, and her presence attracts police as well as S.H.I.E.L.D. agents Nick Fury and Phil Coulson. As they question her, they are fired upon by a Skrull in disguise. Vers pursues the alien, but after a fight aboard a train, loses him — though not without recovering a crystal containing her disjointed extracted memories, which her lead her to a bar in a nearby town. Meanwhile Fury, driving after Vers, discovers a Skrull impersonating Coulson. The alien dies when Fury purposefully crashes the car. Fury follows a lead to the bar with Vers, while Talos impersonates Fury's boss, Keller.

Fury and Vers agree to work together, and using Fury's security clearance visit the secret air base Pegasus that appeared in Vers' deep memory along with the identity of the older woman, Dr. Wendy Lawson. They learn Lawson was a Kree, and died along with her pilot in a 1989 crash while flight-testing her light-speed engine — and that the pilot was Vers. Avoiding capture by "Keller" and S.H.I.E.L.D. agents, Vers and Fury escape in a small cargo jet, with Lawson's cat Goose, adopted by the base, having stowed away. In Louisiana, Fury and Vers speak to Maria Rambeau, Vers' fellow jet pilot (callsign "Photon"), the last person to see Lawson and Vers alive. They fill each other in with the help of Maria's young daughter Monica, and Vers learns she was jet pilot Carol Danvers. Talos, who had tracked her, arrives and asks to speak. Using the black-box recorder of Lawson's crashed plane, Danvers learns an alien spaceship had shot it down — and she now remembers Yon-Rogg killing Lawson, as well as Lawson's warning that the experimental engine's energy-core had to be destroyed to keep it from the wrong hands. Vers shot it and was caught in an ensuing explosion, with her body absorbing the energy. Yon-Rogg brought the amnesiac "Vers" — the remaining portion of "Danvers" on her broken dog tag — to Hala.

Talos explains the Skrulls are simply refugees, and Lawson's engine was to help them escape beyond Kree reach. Using coordinates Lawson had given Danvers, she, along with Fury, Monica, and Goose — who for some reason terrifies Talos — fly their Skrull-modified cargo jet to a cloaked ship in orbit. There they find the source of the energy-core's power, the Tesseract — and some Skrull refugees, including Talos' mate and child. Yon-Rogg's Starforce, having tracked them, captures Danvers and places her in VR before the Supreme Intelligence. Enraged at being used, Danvers fights back, and discovers that a Kree implant that purportedly gave her Kree powers was instead limiting them. Accessing her full force, Danvers escapes, gives the Tesseract to Fury for safekeeping, and battles the Starforce to give Rambeau time to fly herself, Fury, and the Skrulls to Earth — along with Goose, who is not a cat but an alien known as a Flerken that can sprout powerful tentacles and whose insides are a pocket dimension where the Tesseract is placed. Rambeau shoots down a pursuing Minerva. Danvers chases an escaping Yon-Rogg and discovers the extent of her powers, including flight. She singlehandedly destroys multiple ballistic missiles that Kree official Ronan the Accuser, called in by Yon-Rogg, has fired at Earth, and forces Ronan's forces to retreat. On Earth, Danvers quickly defeats Yon-Rogg and sends him back to Hala with a warning to the Supreme Intelligence.

Danvers agrees to help the Skrulls find a new homeworld, giving Fury a modified pager with which to contact her in an emergency. Fury later drafts a proposal for an initiative, changing the name after finding a photo with Danvers' old callsign of "Avenger".

In a present-day mid-credits scene, Danvers meet Avengers Steve Rogers, Natasha Romanoff, Bruce Banner, and James Rhodes, who were monitoring the pager Fury had activated. In an end-credits flashback, Goose coughs up the Tesseract.

All kids have trantrums and misbehave. I always thought it was tooooo waaayy convenient Clark Kent was always an pure angel.
This approach is more realistic. He has powers that go beyond his maturity and understanding of the world. He said he wants to do good, but he is too young and powerful to know what is good. Maybe he is killing all these people because that's what he thinks is good.
When this kid understand (if) the spiritual weight of ending a life, I could totally understand he would not want to do that again.

text: Superman is a fantasy character. But this is really what would likely happen if he came to Earth. Everyone would either worship him, or try to kill him...but everyone would be scared shitless. Any display of super powers, and he would be arrested. If he refused, it would instantly escalate. Very soon, it would be the planet VS him. So far, this looks like a must see movie, especially if you are a comics fan. By the way, imagine you are a 12 year old kid that's bullied at school and you discover you have these godlike powers....any attempt to defend yourself, and people would be seriously hurt, and scared......and then you are a world threat. The only course of action would be to completely hide your abilities for your entire life.
